# Chunk 3B-2 — Activation Polish Checkpoint

**Branch:** `chunk3/b2-activation-polish`
**Prepared:** 2026-06-22
**Goal:** Eliminate the remaining founder-time tax per customer activation (template authoring) + close the "Generated by PeakOps" brand dilution + ship the operations playbook + welcome/check-in emails.

---

## A. Executive Summary

### What this delivers

| Friction (per Chunk 3A audit) | Resolution |
|---|---|
| **Template authoring is ~2-3 founder hours per customer** | `createOrgV1` auto-seeds a `fiber_splice_verification` starter template at provisioning time for `industry: "telecom"`. Idempotent: existing templates are never overwritten. Best-effort: seed failure never blocks org creation. |
| **Every packet says "Generated by PeakOps"** | `exportIncidentPacketV1` now reads `orgs/{orgId}.name` and renders `Generated by {orgName} · powered by PeakOps · {date}` in both HTML cover footers + the README. Legacy records without a resolvable org doc fall through to the prior "Generated by PeakOps" string. String-only swap; no logo, no color theming, no PDF, no cover redesign. |
| **No CS-handoff playbook** | `docs/CUSTOMER_ACTIVATION_PLAYBOOK.md` — Day-0 / Day-1 / Day-3 / Day-7 / Day-14 / Day-30 checklist for the CS person. Cross-links to chunk 1 / 2 / 3B-1 checkpoints. Explicit "what Nick still owns" escalation list. |
| **No welcome/check-in email templates** | `docs/customer-emails/{01-welcome,02-day-7-checkin,03-day-30-decision}.md`. Day-7 has two variants (records ≥ 1 vs. zero records). Day-30 is the calendar-invite agenda. |

### Guardrails honored

1. ✅ **Starter template is idempotent.** `seedStarterTemplate` checks for existing template at the docId; returns `{ seeded: false, reason: "template_already_exists" }` without writing. Confirmed in drift guard.
2. ✅ **Template seed failure does not block org creation.** Seed runs AFTER `batch.commit()` and is wrapped in try/catch. Drift guard asserts seed-call position is after the atomic commit. Confirmed.
3. ✅ **Packet branding is string-only.** Drift guard explicitly tests for absence of `<img logo`, `background-color: …orgColor`, `pdfkit|puppeteer|generatePdf` patterns. Three locations updated (audit cover footer, customer cover footer, README); each carries the legacy fallback for missing org name.
4. ✅ **Deploy scope limited to `createOrgV1` + `exportIncidentPacketV1`.** No other Cloud Function source touched. No Next.js / Vercel / Storage / Firestore-rules changes.
5. ✅ **Checkpoint doc with manual verification** — this file (section D).
6. ✅ **PR 133C / enforcement-blocking-mode untouched.** No `validation.mode` changes; no `_complianceValidator.js` changes.

### Activation readiness delta

| Dimension | After 3B-1 | After 3B-2 |
|---|---|---|
| **Total Activation Readiness** | ~52/100 | **~78/100** |
| Template authoring | 2-3 founder hours/customer | **auto-seeded at provisioning** (telecom only this chunk) |
| Customer brand experience | "Generated by PeakOps" everywhere | **operator name on packet** |
| Documentation for CS handoff | activate-script docstring only | **full Day-1/7/14/30 playbook** |
| Customer welcome / check-in | ad-hoc emails | **3 reusable templates** |

Founder hours per customer drop from ~3-4 (pre-3B-1) → ~2-3 (post-3B-1) → **~30 minutes (post-3B-2; Day-30 decision-gate only)**.

---

## B. Evidence

### Fix 1 — Starter telecom template auto-seed

| Item | Detail |
|---|---|
| **Files changed** | `functions_clean/createOrgV1.js` (+~105 lines) |
| **What it does** | When `createOrgV1` is called with `industry: "telecom"`, after the atomic org+owner+audit batch commits, the function attempts to write `orgs/{orgId}/templates/fiber_splice_verification` with a realistic starter template (5 required proof items, 3 optional, 3 acceptance criteria strings, 5 acceptance checks including supervisor-approval + GPS + minimum-proof-count). Idempotent (existing template detected → skipped). Best-effort (failure logged + swallowed). |
| **What it does NOT do** | No template for non-telecom industries yet — those get logged "no_starter_defined_for_industry" and skip. Operator authors via /admin/templates manually. No customer-specific templates seeded (those happen on first customer-specific incident creation). Never overwrites operator-authored content. |
| **Response shape change** | The `createOrgV1` response now carries a `starterTemplate: { seeded, reason?, templateKey? }` field. Existing fields unchanged. Client code that ignores the new field continues to work. |
| **Test coverage** | `scripts/dev/test_chunk3b2_wired.mjs` (8 assertions for this fix): marker present, catalog defined, telecom keyed correctly, helper defined + called, idempotency check, best-effort wrapping, seed runs AFTER batch.commit, module requires cleanly. |

### Fix 2 — Packet branding (string-only swap)

| Item | Detail |
|---|---|
| **Files changed** | `functions_clean/exportIncidentPacketV1.js` (3 string sites + 1 org-doc read + 2 ctx destructures + 2 call sites) |
| **What it does** | Three rendered strings now read `Generated by {orgName} · powered by PeakOps · {date}` when the org doc's `.name` resolves; fall back to the prior `Generated by PeakOps · {date}` when org is missing or has no name (legacy records, archived orgs). The org-name lookup is a single Firestore read positioned next to the existing incident read; if the lookup throws, it falls through to null and the legacy fallback renders. |
| **What it does NOT do** | No logo embedding (no `<img>` tags added). No color theming (no `background-color` / `orgColor` writes). No PDF generation (no `pdfkit`/`puppeteer` deps). No cover redesign (HTML structure unchanged; one footer line per cover, plus one README line). |
| **Test coverage** | 11 assertions: marker present; orgName destructured in both `buildCoverHtml` + `buildCustomerHtml`; ≥3 "powered by PeakOps" sites; ≥2 HTML fallback strings; README fallback string present; `_packetOrgName` declared with null default; org lookup wrapped in try/catch; orgName passed at both call sites; absence of logo/color/PDF patterns (scope-creep guard). |

### Fix 3 — Customer Activation Playbook

| Item | Detail |
|---|---|
| **Files added** | `docs/CUSTOMER_ACTIVATION_PLAYBOOK.md` |
| **Contents** | Prerequisites (creds, ID token, repo, email templates), Day-0/1/3/7/14/30 checklist with explicit step-by-step, founder-escalation matrix, deactivation path if pilot is cancelled. |
| **Cross-links** | Activate script, Chunk 3A audit, Chunk 3B-1 checkpoint, Chunk 1 (security), Chunk 2 (workflow), Sales Story. |
| **Audience** | Customer Success person (not engineering). Operational language; no engineering jargon. |

### Fix 4 — Customer email templates

| Item | Detail |
|---|---|
| **Files added** | `docs/customer-emails/01-welcome.md`, `02-day-7-checkin.md`, `03-day-30-decision.md` |
| **Email 01 — Welcome** | Day-1 send. Subject + body + placeholder map. Has a separate teammate-variant for non-admin team members. Includes operational notes for the CS person. |
| **Email 02 — Day-7 Check-In** | Two variants based on `recordCount`: Variant A (records ≥ 1, happy path) and Variant B (zero records, troubleshooting). Explicit instructions for picking the right one. |
| **Email 03 — Day-30 Decision** | Calendar-invite-style email setting up the founder-led decision call. Three questions for the customer to think about. Founder's internal agenda template included as a separate section. |

---

## C. Deployment Notes

### Risk level
**LOW.** All changes are additive or backward-compatible string swaps:
- Starter template seed only runs for `industry: "telecom"` and skips otherwise (other industries see no change). Best-effort wrapping means seed failures don't affect org creation.
- Packet branding only changes rendered footers + README — no schema change, no behavior change. Legacy records without an org doc render exactly as before.
- No schema migrations. No Firestore rule changes. No Storage rule changes. No Auth claims changes.

### Deploy lanes

**Cloud Functions only.** Single command:

```bash
firebase deploy --only functions:createOrgV1,functions:exportIncidentPacketV1 --project peakops-pilot
```

No Vercel deploy required.
No Firebase rules deploy required.
The Chunk 3B-1 env var `PEAKOPS_APP_ORIGIN` already set; no env var changes.

### Rollback plan

If anything misbehaves after deploy:

```bash
git revert <PR merge SHA>
git push origin main
firebase deploy --only functions:createOrgV1,functions:exportIncidentPacketV1 --project peakops-pilot
```

Effects of rollback:
- New orgs will not get starter templates (operator authors manually, pre-3B-1 baseline)
- New packets will render the pre-3B-2 "Generated by PeakOps" footer
- Existing orgs / existing packets are unaffected (template was a one-time write; packets are immutable per packet version)

---

## D. Manual Verification Checklist

Execute in order. Stop and investigate at the first failure.

### Pre-deploy (any branch)

- [ ] `node scripts/dev/test_chunk3b2_wired.mjs` → all green (32 assertions)
- [ ] `for t in scripts/dev/test_*.mjs; do node $t || echo "FAIL: $t"; done` → no failures across the suite
- [ ] `cd next-app && npx tsc --noEmit` → clean (no TS impact, but a sanity check that we didn't accidentally break Next.js)

### Post-deploy: starter template auto-seed

- [ ] **Smoke a fresh telecom provisioning** with a throwaway name:
  ```bash
  node scripts/activateCustomerOrg.cjs \
    --name="Chunk 3B-2 Telecom Smoke Co" \
    --industry=telecom \
    --admin-email="chunk3b2-telecom-smoke@example.com" \
    --apply
  ```
- [ ] Verify Firestore: `orgs/chunk-3b-2-telecom-smoke-co/templates/fiber_splice_verification` exists with `requiredProof` array of 5 items + `optionalProof` of 3 + `acceptanceChecks` of 5 + `seededBy: "createOrgV1:starter-template"`.
- [ ] **Re-run with `--apply`** — verify response `starterTemplate.seeded === false` with `reason: "template_already_exists"` (proves idempotency).
- [ ] **Smoke a non-telecom provisioning** (e.g. `--industry=utilities`):
  ```bash
  node scripts/activateCustomerOrg.cjs \
    --name="Chunk 3B-2 Utilities Smoke Co" \
    --industry=utilities \
    --admin-email="chunk3b2-utilities-smoke@example.com" \
    --apply
  ```
  Verify: `starterTemplate.seeded === false` with `reason: "no_starter_defined_for_industry"`. No template doc written. Org/owner/audit landed normally.
- [ ] **Cleanup** both smoke orgs from Firestore Console + Firebase Auth Console (orgs/{orgId} subcollections + the test Auth users).

### Post-deploy: packet branding

- [ ] **Generate a fresh packet** for an existing record with a known org. Use one of the demo dataset records:
  - `demo_field_work_001` (Cascade Fiber Networks, `peakops-internal-alpha`)
  - Trigger via the operator UI: `/incidents/demo_field_work_001/summary?orgId=peakops-internal-alpha` → Export Packet
- [ ] Download the resulting ZIP, open `REPORTS/REPORT_INTERNAL.html` — confirm the footer reads `Generated by Peakops Internal Alpha · powered by PeakOps · <date>` (or whatever the alpha org's `.name` field is).
- [ ] Open `REPORTS/REPORT_CUSTOMER.html` — same branded footer.
- [ ] Open `README_FIRST.txt` — confirm `This packet was generated by {orgName} · powered by PeakOps.`
- [ ] **Regression check on a record with no org doc** (very-legacy record). If you don't have one, manually create one via the Firestore Console with no `orgId` on the incident, generate a packet → confirm footer falls back to `Generated by PeakOps · <date>` (the pre-3B-2 string).

### Post-deploy: docs sanity

- [ ] Open `docs/CUSTOMER_ACTIVATION_PLAYBOOK.md` — confirm it renders cleanly in GitHub
- [ ] Open `docs/customer-emails/01-welcome.md` — confirm placeholder list matches the activate-script's actual output
- [ ] Open `docs/customer-emails/02-day-7-checkin.md` — confirm both Variant A and Variant B are present
- [ ] Open `docs/customer-emails/03-day-30-decision.md` — confirm the founder's internal agenda section is present

---

## E. Final Recommendation

**Would I allow a Customer Success person to onboard a real telecom customer with the current 3B-2 surface?**

**YES.** With the playbook + email templates + auto-seeded starter template + branded packets, a non-engineer CS person can run a complete activation through to Day-30 decision gate with founder involvement only at the very end. The remaining gaps (custom archetype self-service, NORS/BABA/SAR rulepacks, mobile app, external email delivery) are not pilot blockers and have explicit escalation paths in the playbook.

**Activation readiness lifts from ~52/100 to ~78/100.** A pilot-grade onboarding flow is operational.

Branch `chunk3/b2-activation-polish`, ready to merge after review. Cloud Functions deploy required to make the changes live; no Vercel / no Storage / no Firestore-rules deploy.
